message:
fix(ci): use ubuntu-24.04 runners for Linux release builds

ort-sys prebuilt ONNX Runtime binaries require glibc ≥2.38 (C23
__isoc23_strtol, __cxa_call_terminate). Ubuntu 22.04 only ships
glibc 2.35, causing linker failures. Switch to ubuntu-24.04 (glibc
2.39) which satisfies the ort-sys floor.

The minimum glibc for prebuilt binaries is now 2.39. Users on older
distros should build from source.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -33,13 +33,14 @@ jobs:
       fail-fast: false
       matrix:
         include:
-          # Linux x86_64 — use ubuntu-22.04 (glibc 2.35) for broader compatibility
-          - os: ubuntu-22.04
+          # Linux x86_64 — ubuntu-24.04 (glibc 2.39); ort-sys prebuilt ONNX
+          # Runtime binaries require glibc ≥2.38, so 22.04 cannot link.
+          - os: ubuntu-24.04
             target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
             artifact_name: cass
             asset_name: cass-linux-amd64
-          # Linux ARM64 — use ubuntu-22.04-arm (glibc 2.35) for broader compatibility
-          - os: ubuntu-22.04-arm
+          # Linux ARM64 — same glibc constraint as x86_64
+          - os: ubuntu-24.04-arm
             target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
             artifact_name: cass
             asset_name: cass-linux-arm64
